title: Status indicator is color-only
description: Connection status conveyed solely by dot color (red/green). No text, no aria-label, no role. Invisible to colorblind users and screen readers. Location: editor.css:106-117, editor.ts:567-568. WCAG 1.4.1 Level A violation. Fix: add aria-label reflecting state or add visible text.

title: Allow choosing template in the editor interface
description: Add UI for selecting and loading templates directly from the canvas editor. Considerations: when to show (on canvas open? via toolbar button? as empty-state?), how to preview templates before loading, whether it replaces current content or opens new canvas. Currently templates can only be loaded via MCP tool calls.

title: Connectors/arrows between objects
description: Add programmatic arrow connectors that link two canvas objects. Essential for flowcharts and diagrams. New MCP tool sketch_add_connector with params: from_object_id, to_object_id, style (straight/curved), color, label. Connectors should update position when source/target objects move. Excalidraw and tldraw both support this. Consider Fabric.js line + arrowhead approach, or a custom connector class that listens to object:moving events.

title: Add photo capture to mobile input page
description: Add camera/photo input to the mobile page using <input type='file' accept='image/*' capture='environment'>. When user takes/selects a photo: resize to reasonable dimensions (max 800px), convert to base64, send via WebSocket, and add as FabricImage to the canvas at a sensible position. This enables using the phone camera to add real-world images to the canvas.

title: Add WebSocket message types for mobile text updates
description: Add new message types to support mobile-to-canvas text sync: ServerMessage: update_textbox (target object index/id + new text), ClientMessage: canvas_textboxes (list of editable textbox objects with index, current text, label/position for the mobile form). The mobile page needs to identify which textbox to update -- use object index in canvas.getObjects() array or a custom id property.

title: Element-level get/update/delete operations
description: Currently can only add elements or replace entire SVG. Add tools to get individual object properties, update specific objects by ID, and delete objects. Enables precise AI editing without redrawing everything.

title: Label text contrast fails WCAG AA
description: Toolbar label color #868e96 on white background has ~3.5:1 contrast ratio, below 4.5:1 minimum for 12px text. Location: editor.css:73. WCAG 1.4.3 Level AA violation. Fix: darken to at least #6c757d (4.7:1).

title: Build mobile input page at /mobile/:canvas
description: Serve a lightweight HTML page (no Fabric.js) at /mobile/:canvas_name. The page should: (1) connect via WebSocket to the same canvas session, (2) request canvas JSON to discover editable Textbox objects (non-locked, type=Textbox), (3) render a simple form with labeled text inputs for each editable textbox, (4) on input change, send updates back via WebSocket to update the corresponding canvas object. Design for mobile-first: large touch targets, simple layout, works on phone/iPad Safari.

title: Touch targets below 44px minimum
description: Buttons 32x32, color inputs 28x28, number input 48x28 -- all below 44px minimum. Location: editor.css:43-46, 80-86, 89-96. WCAG 2.5.8 Level AAA / 2.5.5 Level AA violation. Fix: increase button size to 44px or add padding for hit area.

title: Font inconsistency across templates
description: AEIOU template uses serif for column headers while all others use sans-serif. No consistent type system. Location: aeiou.json vs other template JSONs. Fix: standardize on one font family or make configurable.

title: Template editable textboxes are invisible
description: Editable Textbox objects contain only a single space, render at ~16px height, have no background or border. Users cannot distinguish editable regions from empty space. Location: all 4 template JSON files. Fix: add light background, subtle border, or dashed outline. Set minimum height.

title: No focus indicators on toolbar buttons
description: No :focus-visible styles defined. Keyboard-only users cannot see which button is focused. Location: editor.css:42-55. WCAG 2.4.7 Level AA violation. Fix: add #toolbar button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #339af0; outline-offset: 2px; }

title: Add screenshot/visual feedback tool
description: Let the AI see what's on the canvas. Add sketch_get_screenshot tool that captures the canvas as a PNG image and returns it. This closes the feedback loop -- AI can draw, see the result, and refine iteratively. Both Excalidraw (yctimlin) and tldraw MCP have this. #1 competitive gap.

title: All colors hard-coded -- no CSS custom properties
description: Every color is a literal hex value across editor.css and editor.ts. Cannot theme, add dark mode, or adjust for accessibility. Fix: extract to CSS variables on :root (--bg-primary, --border-color, --text-primary, --accent).

title: Describe/inspect canvas scene
description: Add sketch_describe_scene tool returning structured info about canvas contents: object count, types, positions, dimensions, text content. Lets AI reason about current state without needing a screenshot image.

title: Undo stack stores full JSON snapshots
description: Every state change serializes entire canvas to JSON, storing up to 50 snapshots. With complex canvases this consumes significant memory. Location: editor.ts:306-313. Fix: consider structural sharing or delta-based undo.

title: Viewport and zoom control
description: Add MCP tools for controlling the canvas viewport: sketch_zoom (level or fit-to-content), sketch_scroll_to (x,y or object_id), sketch_auto_fit (zoom to show all content with padding). Fabric.js supports canvas.setZoom(), canvas.viewportTransform, and canvas.zoomToPoint(). Excalidraw and tldraw both offer viewport control. Enables AI to frame the view after drawing.

title: Bind server to 0.0.0.0 for LAN access
description: Change Express/WebSocket server bind from localhost to 0.0.0.0 so mobile devices on the same network can connect. One-line change in canvas-manager.ts where the HTTP server listens. Add optional --host flag or environment variable to control this (default 0.0.0.0). Security note: document that this exposes the editor on the local network.

title: Export canvas to PNG image
description: Add sketch_export_image tool to generate downloadable/shareable raster output (PNG). Fabric.js has canvas.toDataURL('png'). Return as base64 or save to file and return path.

title: No dark mode support
description: No @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) or manual toggle. Canvas background hard-coded to #ffffff in JS. Causes eye strain in dark environments. Fix: after extracting CSS variables, add dark theme variant.

title: Toolbar buttons have no ARIA labels
description: Buttons use title for tooltips but have no aria-label. Screen readers see empty buttons with only SVG content. Location: toolbar.ts:68-73. WCAG 4.1.2 Level A violation. Fix: add aria-label matching tool name to each button.

title: Keyboard shortcuts conflict with canvas text editing
description: Single-key shortcuts (V, R, E, T, L, A, D, X) only guard against INPUT/TEXTAREA tags, not Fabric.js text editing. Typing in canvas text could switch tools. Location: toolbar.ts:149-162. Fix: check canvas.getActiveObject() instanceof IText && isEditing.

title: Canvas element has no accessible role or description
description: The canvas element has no role, aria-label, or tabindex. Primary interactive surface is completely inaccessible. Location: editor.ts:29-39. WCAG 4.1.2 Level A violation. Fix: add role=img with aria-label or role=application.

title: Mobile input page with QR code
description: Split into subtasks: sketch-mcp-server-e7b (bind 0.0.0.0), sketch-mcp-server-ypb (QR code), sketch-mcp-server-3fj (WS message types), sketch-mcp-server-545 (mobile page), sketch-mcp-server-2i5 (photo capture)

title: Show QR code with LAN URL
description: When server starts, detect local IP (os.networkInterfaces), generate QR code for http://<local-ip>:<port>/mobile/<canvas>. Display QR in two places: (1) terminal output on server start using a terminal QR library (qrcode-terminal or similar), (2) optionally in the editor UI corner. Include the canvas name in the URL so the mobile page connects to the right session.
